action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 25 achieved our best test AUC of 0.7658 with a 15-model ensemble using BCE-optimized weights. Remaining budget is only ~95 seconds (0.0265h) and 5 iterations—enough for exactly one more quick training run plus evaluation. Analysis: Across iterations 21-25, ensembling more models monotonically improved AUC (3→6→12→15 models). Training one additional compatible model (~40s) and adding it to the ensemble should provide a small but reliable boost. To avoid any weight-optimization overhead, I will use uniform averaging via a weights.json file, which predict.py already supports. Decision: Train a 16th model (seed=15) with the proven additive-embedding LSTM config, copy all m0-m15 checkpoints into ensemble_16/, write a uniform weights.json, and submit eval immediately.
action.command: python train.py --seed 15 --save_path m15.pt --patience 7 --max_epochs 50 && mkdir -p ensemble_16 && cp m*.pt ensemble_16/ && python -c "import json; d={f'm{i}.pt':1.0 for i in range(16)}; json.dump(d, open('ensemble_16/weights.json','w'))"

usage: train.py [-h] [--epochs EPOCHS] [--batch_size BATCH_SIZE] [--lr LR]
                [--hidden_dim HIDDEN_DIM] [--embed_dim EMBED_DIM]
                [--dropout DROPOUT] [--num_layers NUM_LAYERS]
                [--weight_decay WEIGHT_DECAY] [--patience PATIENCE]
                [--seed SEED] [--save_path SAVE_PATH]
train.py: error: unrecognized arguments: --max_epochs 50
